danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threat density; heightened danger zones

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Chaotic level design; enemy AI becomes unpredictable

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss battle mechanics; ultimate threat manifestation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mutant power bar empty; character animation reflects weakness

environmental_storytelling.example: Danger Room location establishes training scenario; virus intrusion drives entire plot
Incremental villain encounters build toward Magneto revelation as true orchestrator
Each playable character's unique power set (Wolverine claws, Gambit staff, Cyclops blasts, Nightcrawler teleport) communicated through visual moveset differentiation
Storm, Rogue, Iceman, Archangel available for support; Jean Grey rescue mechanic shows team hierarchy and safety net

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Claw attacks enhance punch strength; mid-air special attacks available
Healing factor visually regenerates life bar over time
Bo staff weapon; charged cards track and pursue enemies autonomously
Optic blast directional attacks (A button); rebounding blast mechanic for environmental interaction
Teleportation ability allows level skip; can transport secondary character
Boss battle mechanics; represents endgame threat manifestation
Cameo appearance; ironically assists player in-flight despite villain status
Visually appears to retrieve fallen characters; safety net mechanic

notes: X-Men (1993) uses the Danger Room training scenario as its primary world-state communicator. Visual signals rely heavily on character animation states (power bar depletion/regeneration) and environmental hazards (virus-corrupted facility) rather than explicit UI elements. The game's faction communication is character-focused: playable X-Men are distinguished by their unique visual movesets, while antagonists (Magneto, various villains) are presented as discrete boss encounters. World state progression is linear and narrative-driven: virus infection → villain encounters → Magneto reveal. The game communicates danger primarily through enemy placement complexity and boss difficulty spikes rather than environmental color-coding. Support characters provide visual reassurance through appearance mechanics. No explicit faction color-coding or sigil system is documented.
